title: Pack submission initiation
text_ieee830: The Workbench shall accept applicant submission of an Operational Authorisation Pack via web portal, with drag-and-drop file upload capability for artefacts (PDF, XLSX, DOCX format). Upon submission, the Workbench shall timestamp the submission and initiate Artefact Detector component.

title: Pack artefact detection (automated)
text_ieee830: The Artefact Detector shall parse submitted files and identify document type (ConOps, SORA, Safety Case, etc.) via filename pattern matching and header inspection. For each identified artefact, the system shall record presence/absence, file size, and page count. The system shall output a RAG matrix (Red/Amber/Green status) per artefact against mandatory-artefact list.

title: Pack completeness assessment (Quick Gate)
text_ieee830: The Workbench shall, within 10 minutes of submission, run Quick Gate (METER-001): Artefact Detector maps identified files to 8 mandatory artefacts and generates RAG matrix. If any artefact is Red (missing) or Amber (present but incomplete per page-count thresholds: ConOps >= 5 pages, SORA >= 3 pages, Safety Case >= 10 pages, etc.), Quick Gate FAILS and case enters 'return-for-clarification' state. If all artefacts are Green, Quick Gate PASSES and case advances to Thorough Gate.

title: Pack completeness assessment (Thorough Gate)
text_ieee830: Upon Quick Gate PASS, case is assigned to CAA case officer who conducts Thorough Gate (METER-002): manual section-by-section audit of each artefact (ConOps sections: swarm composition, phases, failure modes, worst-credible contingencies; SORA sections: hazard tree, swarm interdependencies, controls; Safety Case sections: assumption validation, argument sufficiency, evidence cross-references). Case officer marks each section Present-Substantive, Present-Vague, or Absent. If >= 90% sections are Present-Substantive, Thorough Gate PASSES; case advances to SORA Assessment panel.

title: Return-for-clarification notification
text_ieee830: If Quick Gate or Thorough Gate fails, Workbench shall notify applicant within 1 business day with itemized list of missing/incomplete artefacts by category (ConOps section X, SORA missing entirely, Safety Case section Y too brief, etc.). Applicant shall have 14 days to resubmit revised pack.

title: Pack submission versioning
text_ieee830: The Workbench shall maintain immutable version history of all pack submissions (original submission, resubmission 1, resubmission 2, etc.). Each version shall be timestamped and stored in evidence archive with SHA256 hash. Case history shall display which version was accepted for case opening and reasons for prior rejections.

title: Case opening upon pack acceptance
text_ieee830: Upon Thorough Gate PASS, Workbench shall automatically transition case from Draft status to Open status, generate case file (unique ID, applicant name, trial location, classification level), and notify assigned SORA assessor. CAA case officer shall add case to SORA Assessment queue with estimated review date.

title: Swarm-aware SORA evidence reception
text_ieee830: SORA Assessor shall receive applicant-submitted swarm-aware SORA evidence document addressing: (1) inter-vehicle coordination dependencies (how aircraft coordinate, what happens if one fails); (2) emergent risks specific to multi-agent operation (separation-loss cascades, deadlock, Byzantine failures); (3) documented hazard analysis (FTA, STAMP, or HARA explicitly mapping swarm-specific failure modes). Evidence shall reference JARUS SORA v2.5 methodology or published swarm-aware SORA supplement (when available).

title: SORA swarm evidence validation
text_ieee830: SORA Assessor shall evaluate whether swarm evidence defensibly addresses: (1) JARUS v2.5 methodology applied to swarm context (hazard identification, risk classification); (2) inter-aircraft failure dependencies documented (does failure of aircraft A increase risk to aircraft B?); (3) controls proposed for identified swarm-specific hazards (formation separation enforcement, leader loss recovery, Byzantine detection). Assessor shall issue: ACCEPT (evidence sufficient), RFC (return for clarification with specific gaps), or REFUSE (evidence fundamentally inadequate).

title: SAIL/OSO determination
text_ieee830: Upon swarm evidence ACCEPT, SORA Assessor shall determine Specific Assurance and Integrity Level (SAIL) and Operational Safety Objectives (OSO) that explicitly reference swarm coordination constraints. Example OSO: 'Separation maintained during coordinated evasion maneuver shall be >= 50 meters horizontally and 20 meters vertically; formation shall not deadlock (all aircraft movement ceases) for > 10 seconds.' SAIL and OSO shall be documented and forwarded to safety-case reviewer.

title: ConOps swarm composition specification
text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide ConOps document specifying: (1) swarm composition (number of aircraft, types, roles: lead, follower, observer); (2) communication architecture (mesh, star, hierarchical); (3) decision authority per operational phase (who decides: ground crew, lead aircraft, peer-vote consensus?). Safety-case reviewer shall validate that roles, communication, and authority match swarm decision-making architecture (REQ008).

title: ConOps operational phases
text_ieee830: ConOps shall specify operational phases with crew decision points: launch (pre-flight checks, system arm), transit (waypoint navigation, formation hold), contingency (loss of leader, C2 loss, encounter evasion), recovery (descent, landing, system disarm). For each phase, identify nominal behavior thresholds and crew intervention triggers.

title: Worst-credible behavior coverage
text_ieee830: Safety-case reviewer shall validate that ConOps explicitly addresses all worst-credible behavior scenarios (most severe single-failure or environmental excursion reasonably foreseeable). Worst-credible scenarios include: GPS loss, C2 link loss, geofence breach, airspace intrusion, low battery, crew incapacitation. For each scenario, ConOps shall specify crew procedure or containment system response that prevents safety-critical outcome.

title: ODD boundary specification
text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide Operational Design Domain (ODD) specifying operating conditions: wind speed <= 15 knots, temperature -5°C to +30°C, GPS accuracy better than 5 meters, daylight hours only (or night operations if equipped with lighting). ODD defines the envelope within which swarm behavior is predictable per ConOps and autonomy architecture.

title: TDA 3D airspace definition
text_ieee830: Applicant shall submit Temporary Danger Area (TDA) request to Airspace Regulator specifying 3D airspace volume: (1) latitude/longitude polygon (trial corridor); (2) altitude bands (floor and ceiling AGL); (3) time window (date range, daylight hours, nights if applicable). TDA shall be formatted per ICAO Annex 15 NOTAM requirements.

title: TDA consultation evidence
text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide evidence of consultation with: (1) ATSU (Air Traffic Service Unit) confirming TDA monitoring capability and NOTAM publication; (2) airspace users (e.g., commercial routes, helicopter operators) acknowledging TDA constraints; (3) affected landowners (property owners below trial corridor) with notice of flight operations. Consultation documentation shall include dates of contact, responses received, and any conditions imposed by consulted parties.

title: Geofence boundary tolerance specification
text_ieee830: The Workbench geofence monitor shall define 3D boundary with tolerance ±10 meters (i.e., aircraft position may drift 10m beyond advertised boundary before containment triggers). This tolerance accounts for GPS uncertainty (±5m typical), wind gust deflection, and sensor latency. Boundary coordinates shall be entered with confidence interval (e.g., 'boundary 51.3°N ± 0.001°, aircraft position must remain within this latitude').

title: Geofence breach detection
text_ieee830: Onboard geofence monitor shall poll GNSS at 10 Hz minimum. If aircraft position exceeds boundary by > 10 meters for > 2 seconds (hysteresis filter to avoid false triggers), system shall declare geofence-breach condition and initiate containment action within 500 milliseconds.

title: Primary containment action (Return-to-Launch)
text_ieee830: Upon geofence breach detection, onboard autonomy shall prioritize Return-to-Launch (RTL) if GPS signal confidence > 95%. RTL shall compute safe descent path to final landing location and execute maneuver autonomously (without requiring ground crew input). RTL shall respect ODD boundaries (wind limits, terrain clearance).

title: Failover containment action (climb-and-hold)
text_ieee830: If GPS confidence drops below 95% (signal loss), onboard autonomy shall execute failover: climb to 500 feet AGL minimum and maintain level flight (no descent) until GPS recovers or crew intervention occurs. Failover preserves safe altitude above terrain while awaiting GPS recovery or crew manual landing.

title: Crew containment alert
text_ieee830: Upon geofence breach detection, Workbench shall generate crew alert (audio tone 60+ dB, visual indicator on HUD) within 1 second of breach. Alert shall indicate breach location (direction and distance from boundary) and containment action initiated (RTL or climb-and-hold). Crew shall acknowledge alert and monitor containment action completion.

title: C2 link quality telemetry
text_ieee830: Spectrum Regulator shall authorize C2 link frequency allocation and power limits. Applicant shall instrument C2 system to continuously transmit real-time link quality telemetry (RSSI: received signal strength indicator in dBm, BER: bit error rate as percentage) to ground crew display and Workbench logger. Link quality shall be recorded at minimum 1 Hz sampling rate.

title: C2 link loss detection
text_ieee830: C2 link loss shall be detected when signal quality (RSSI or BER) drops below minimum threshold for > 1 second. Minimum threshold shall be determined during pre-trial testing and documented in trial authorization. Upon detection, onboard autonomy shall execute failover: if inter-vehicle links available, swarm coordinates evasion autonomously; if inter-vehicle links also unavailable, each aircraft independently executes Return-to-Launch.

title: C2 link survivability demonstration
text_ieee830: Applicant shall demonstrate through simulation and flight test that swarm remains safe (maintains separation, respects geofence, avoids collision) for minimum 5 minutes after C2 link loss, sufficient for crew to re-establish link or initiate independent termination. Survivability test shall document: time until GPS-based navigation fails (if applicable), time until battery/fuel margin insufficient for RTL, time until crew manual takeover required.

title: Inter-vehicle link coordination
text_ieee830: For swarm coordination during C2 loss, inter-vehicle links shall provide real-time peer-to-peer communication (aircraft-to-aircraft): position sharing (each aircraft broadcasts location and velocity), decision coordination (consensus protocol for avoiding evasion conflicts), heartbeat (periodic 'alive' signal to detect failed aircraft). Inter-vehicle link design and frequency allocation shall be approved by Spectrum Regulator and documented in trial authorization.

title: DAA sensor detection range
text_ieee830: Each aircraft in swarm shall be equipped with onboard Detect-and-Avoid (DAA) sensor (radar or electro-optical) with minimum detection range 500 meters, update rate 1 Hz. DAA sensor shall operate independent of C2 link (autonomous on-aircraft function). Applicant shall provide evidence of DAA sensor performance (detection probability, false-alarm rate) per DO-365 Minimum Operational Performance Standards.

title: Cooperative encounter resolution
text_ieee830: For cooperative encounters (intruder transmits ADS-B or FLARM signal indicating position/velocity), swarm de-confliction algorithm shall: (1) receive intruder position via sensor and broadcast data, (2) compute relative bearing and closing rate, (3) select recommended avoidance maneuver (climb, descend, turn left/right) that provides minimum 100-meter separation, (4) broadcast maneuver to all swarm aircraft via inter-vehicle links, (5) enforce agreed maneuver execution within 2 seconds.

title: Non-cooperative encounter resolution
text_ieee830: For non-cooperative encounters (intruder does not transmit ADS-B/FLARM), DAA system shall: (1) detect intruder via radar/EO sensor, (2) compute relative motion, (3) issue crew-advisory maneuver (preferred direction and magnitude to achieve 100-meter separation), (4) record decision rationale and alternative maneuvers considered in log. Crew shall evaluate advisory and execute manually or allow autonomous execution per trial procedures.

title: Encounter resolution predictability
text_ieee830: Encounter resolution is predictable when: (1) same encounter geometry (relative bearing, range, closing rate) always produces same recommended maneuver (deterministic, not probabilistic); (2) all swarm aircraft follow recommended maneuver within 2-second execution window (no divergence); (3) resulting maneuver is foreseeable to all trial participants (documented in operational procedures). Non-deterministic (ML-based, probabilistic) encounter algorithms are not acceptable unless validated against 1000+ diverse encounter scenarios with 99%+ determinism rate.

title: Encounter resolution failure criteria
text_ieee830: Failure conditions for encounter resolution: collision (aircraft or swarm member separation < 50 meters), loss of separation (separation < 100 meters between any two aircraft or between aircraft and intruder), crew inability to monitor encounter outcome. Upon any failure, trial shall be immediately suspended and incident investigation initiated (REQ012).

title: Swarm decision-making architecture options
text_ieee830: Applicant shall document swarm decision-making architecture and justify selection of one of three design patterns: (1) Centralized (ground crew or ground-based AI agent makes all swarm decisions, aircraft execute commands), (2) Distributed (aircraft share state via inter-vehicle links, peer-vote consensus selects action), (3) Hierarchical (lead aircraft decides, follower aircraft execute). Each architecture has different failure modes and requires different assurance evidence (REQ008).

title: Swarm decision authority specification
text_ieee830: Applicant shall explicitly specify which entity has decision authority in swarm: (1) ground crew (crew commands transmitted via C2), (2) onboard autonomy (programmed decision rules), (3) peer consensus (aircraft vote on action). Authority shall be defined per operational scenario (nominal, loss of leader, link loss, encounter evasion). Autonomy reviewer shall verify authority specification is consistent with meaningful human control requirement (REQ010).

title: Swarm communication topology
text_ieee830: Applicant shall document swarm communication topology: (1) mesh (all aircraft communicate with all others — high redundancy, higher latency), (2) star (all aircraft communicate with lead aircraft — lower latency, single-point-of-failure if lead loses comms), (3) line (leader to second, second to third, etc. — serial communication, longest latency). Topology choice affects resilience to inter-vehicle link loss and shall be justified in architecture specification.

title: Emergent behavior failure modes
text_ieee830: Applicant shall identify potential emergent behaviors (unintended collective behaviors arising from inter-aircraft interactions): formation deadlock (all aircraft movement ceases), oscillation (formation bounces up-down-up-down without damping), cascade separation-loss (one aircraft's evasion maneuver causes follower to lose separation, triggering second evasion, widening gap). For each identified emergent-failure mode, applicant shall propose detection mechanism (onboard monitoring, ground observation, automated alerting) and recovery procedure.

title: Emergent behavior verification evidence
text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide emergent-behavior evidence through three methods: (1) Formal Verification (model checking or theorem proving proving absence of undesired emergent behaviors in state-machine representation), (2) Monte Carlo Simulation (minimum 1,000 trials per scenario with environmental noise injection, demonstrating behavior variance bounds), (3) HITL Testing (hardware-in-the-loop simulation with human operators testing formation behaviors, contingency responses, loss-of-leader recovery). Results shall bound probability of undesired emergent behaviors (e.g., 'deadlock occurs in < 0.1% of trials; separation-loss cascade depth bounded at 2 aircraft').

title: AMLAS case structure
text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide Assurance Case for Machine Learning in Autonomous Systems (AMLAS) per Assure ML CTC (www.assuranceml.org). AMLAS case shall document: (1) safety requirements levied on ML components (e.g., 'swarm de-confliction must execute in <500ms with <1% error rate'), (2) training data governance (data source, labeling process, distribution verification), (3) model architecture (layers, parameters, non-linearities), (4) robustness testing (adversarial examples, sensor-noise injection, out-of-distribution detection), (5) failure modes (model confidence collapse, out-of-distribution triggers, performance degradation under environmental stress). AMLAS case structure shall follow GSN (Goal Structured Notation) or CAF (Confidence Assurance Framework).

title: Frozen-model manifest documentation
text_ieee830: For each ML model deployed in trial, applicant shall provide Frozen-Model Manifest documenting: (1) model version ID and release date, (2) training dataset hash value (SHA256) enabling post-incident reconstruction, (3) hyperparameters (learning rate, regularization, architecture details), (4) performance metrics (validation accuracy, F1 score, confusion matrix for safety-critical decision classes), (5) operational constraints (input domain thresholds, confidence thresholds for model predictions), (6) explicit immutability confirmation (model weights and architecture shall remain unchanged throughout trial duration, minimum 2 years). Manifest shall be encrypted and stored with copies held by CAA and applicant.

title: ML model explainability techniques
text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide post-hoc explainability evidence demonstrating sufficient transparency for forensic investigation. For deep learning models: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis showing feature importance contributions to individual decisions, decision-tree approximations of learned decision boundaries, or mechanistic interpretability exploration. For traditional ML (decision trees, linear models): direct interpretation of model structure. Forensic explainability does not require perfect reconstruction of neural-network internals but must enable post-incident analysis: identify which inputs triggered anomalous output, determine if model was operating within training-distribution, link decision to model version per Frozen-Model Manifest.

title: Crew situational awareness update frequency
text_ieee830: Crew shall receive swarm state updates (position, airspeed, altitude, link status, autonomous decisions of each swarm member) at minimum 1 Hz frequency via HUD, dashboard, or voice notification. Update frequency shall be sufficient for crew to maintain continuous situational awareness and intervene before safety-critical outcomes (> 5-second intervention time window per REQ010).

title: Crew override authority and latency
text_ieee830: Crew shall retain authority to override autonomous decisions at all times. Override mechanism shall include: (1) dedicated control input (buttons, switches, voice command) for safety-critical functions (swarm termination, return-to-launch, emergency descent), (2) override latency < 2 seconds from crew command to system response (e.g., crew presses 'STOP SWARM' button; within 2 seconds, all aircraft engines cut or parachutes deploy). Latency shall be verified through HITL testing and flight test with independent observer timing crew response.

title: Crew intervention time windows
text_ieee830: Crew shall have minimum intervention time windows to perceive off-nominal events and intervene before safety-critical outcomes: (1) >= 5 seconds for evasion maneuvers (crew detects threat via DAA warning, selects maneuver direction, system executes), (2) >= 10 seconds for return-to-launch initiation (crew recognizes deteriorating conditions, decides RTL is necessary, system begins descent), (3) >= 2 seconds for emergency termination (crew recognizes uncontrolled situation, activates independent termination, system cuts motors). Intervention time windows shall be validated through simulator testing and worst-credible-scenario analysis (ConOps REQ003).

title: Crew workload assessment (NASA-TLX)
text_ieee830: Applicant shall conduct pre-trial workload assessment using NASA-TLX (Task Load Index) or equivalent cognitive-load measurement instrument. Assessment shall measure crew workload in simulator scenarios representing: (1) nominal operations (formation flight, waypoint navigation), (2) contingency operations (loss-of-leader recovery, C2 link loss, encounter evasion). Target workload: <75% of maximum cognitive capacity during nominal operations, <85% during contingency. Results shall be documented and submitted to CAA Human Factors Inspector for approval before trial start.

title: Crew take-over simulator practice
text_ieee830: Crew shall complete minimum 10 practice take-over sessions in HITL simulator covering: (1) assumed-attitude recovery (crew receives blind gyro indicator and must regain awareness of swarm formation state within 30 seconds), (2) formation-separation recovery (crew detects separation loss and must issue manual maneuvers to restore spacing), (3) autonomous-decision-override (crew overrides autonomy system and manually commands swarm to new waypoint). Each session shall be logged with date, scenario, and instructor evaluation (pass/fail). Instructor shall confirm crew achieves >= 95% success rate across all 10 sessions before trial approval.

title: Telemetry recording (aircraft state)
text_ieee830: Workbench shall record aircraft state telemetry at 10 Hz minimum throughout each trial flight: position (latitude, longitude, altitude), velocity (north/east/down), attitude (roll, pitch, yaw), acceleration (linear and angular), engine thrust (or propeller RPM), battery voltage and current. All samples shall be timestamped (GPS time reference, nanosecond precision) and logged to tamper-evident container (encrypted storage with separate integrity hash).

title: Telemetry recording (autonomy decisions)
text_ieee830: Workbench shall record autonomy decision logs at 1 Hz minimum: command issued (waypoint, descent rate, evasion maneuver), decision rationale (rule or ML-model inference leading to command), confidence score (if applicable), alternative commands considered and rejected. Logs shall enable post-incident forensics: determine why system issued a particular command and whether command was appropriate given system state.

title: Telemetry recording (link quality)
text_ieee830: Workbench shall record C2 and inter-vehicle link quality at 1 Hz minimum: RSSI (received signal strength indicator, dBm), BER (bit error rate, percentage), packet loss rate, latency (round-trip time). Logs shall enable post-incident forensics: determine if link was degrading before failure event, whether degradation triggered failover behavior, whether crew was aware of deteriorating link quality.

title: Telemetry recording (crew actions)
text_ieee830: Workbench shall log all crew inputs with timestamp: control inputs (buttons, switches, stick movements), verbal commands (radio transmissions, voice control activations), alert acknowledgments (crew responding to Workbench warnings). Crew voice logs shall be encrypted and treated as sensitive personal data (GDPR compliant): retained only if incident occurs; deleted 90 days post-flight if no incident. Crew shall be informed at mission briefing that voice is being recorded for safety investigation purposes only.

title: Telemetry recording (video & audio)
text_ieee830: Workbench shall record: (1) forward-looking video from lead aircraft (minimum 30 fps, minimum 1080p resolution) showing flight path and adjacent airspace for traffic awareness, (2) downlink video from ground-based operator camera (if applicable, showing trial area from ground perspective), (3) intercom audio from crew (minimum 16 kHz sampling, stereo for multi-crew operations). Video/audio logs shall be time-synchronized to telemetry via GPS time-of-day stamp, enabling correlation of crew actions with aircraft state during incident investigation.

title: MOR trigger identification
text_ieee830: MOR (Mandatory Occurrence Report) shall be filed upon detection of reportable events: (1) accident (any unplanned impact causing injury or structural damage), (2) serious incident (near-miss with < 100-meter separation from third-party aircraft or ground infrastructure), (3) loss-of-separation event (swarm member separation < 100 meters unintentionally), (4) unintended geofence breach (aircraft exits TDA boundary outside planned experiment), (5) autonomous-system anomaly (behavior inconsistent with documented design, indicative of control-system defect), (6) crew incapacitation (medical event disabling crew during operations), (7) unrecovered off-nominal event (crew unable to recover from failure scenario within expected time window). Event triggers shall be pre-briefed to crew and ground observers.

title: MOR compilation and submission
text_ieee830: Upon MOR-reportable event detection, applicant shall compile incident report within 72 hours containing: (1) incident narrative (what happened, when, where, who involved), (2) immediate cause (why did event occur: equipment failure, crew error, environmental factor, software defect?), (3) contributing factors (were there preceding failures or degradation?), (4) recorded evidence links (telemetry files, video, crew statement, photograph), (5) immediate corrective actions taken (swarm terminated, investigation initiated, etc.). Report shall be submitted to CAA Safety Investigation department with evidence attachment (encrypted archive containing telemetry, video, logs).

title: DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment)
text_ieee830: Applicant shall conduct DPIA per GDPR Article 35, identifying: (1) personal data collected (crew names, observer identities, potentially incidental recording of ground personnel faces), (2) processing purposes (safety investigation, regulatory oversight, technical analysis), (3) data retention periods (2+ years or until investigation closes), (4) data-subject rights (right to access, right to erasure upon request after investigation closes), (5) privacy risks (accidental exposure of crew voice recordings, unintended capture of third-party ground personnel). DPIA shall document privacy controls (voice-log encryption, access restriction to authorized investigators, automated redaction of civilian faces in video before public release).

title: Insurance coverage minimum
text_ieee830: Applicant shall obtain third-party liability insurance covering: (1) bodily injury (death or injury to ground personnel below trial corridor), (2) property damage (damage to third-party buildings, vehicles, infrastructure), (3) environmental harm (contamination, wildlife disturbance claims). Minimum coverage: 1 million GBP per occurrence, 5 million GBP annual aggregate. Insurance policy shall not exclude BVLOS or swarm operations. Certificate of insurance shall be provided to CAA prior to trial start and remain active throughout trial duration.

title: Ground environment survey scope
text_ieee830: Applicant shall commission independent ground environment survey assessing trial area within 10 km radius of planned operations. Survey shall be conducted by licensed surveyor or environmental consultant and identify: (1) critical ground infrastructure (power lines, communication towers, railways, roads, water pipelines) with GPS coordinates and criticality classification, (2) population density (buildings, schools, hospitals, campgrounds) by district with estimated occupancy, (3) environmental sensitivity (water sources, protected habitats, cultural heritage sites) per regulatory register, (4) noise-sensitive receptors (residences, hospitals, schools) identified by occupancy type, (5) terrain features and wind patterns affecting aircraft behavior.

title: Ground survey risk mitigation
text_ieee830: For each identified ground hazard (power line within 5 km, hospital within 2 km, protected habitat nearby), applicant shall propose mitigation: (1) reduced altitude (stay above 500 feet to clear power lines), (2) flight-path adjustment (avoid corridor near hospital, use alternate route), (3) noise baffles or operational restrictions (no night operations near residential areas), (4) observer deployment (ground personnel stationed at critical points to watch for hazards). Applicant shall provide risk acceptance rationale for hazards where mitigation is not feasible, documented and approved by CAA Authorisation Officer.

title: Crew competency domains
text_ieee830: Crew shall demonstrate competency across three domains: (1) General Airmanship (private pilot license or equivalent, current medical certificate, minimum 500 flight hours VLOS or BVLOS), (2) BVLOS Swarm Operations (completion of approved training program minimum 20 hours covering swarm behavior, coordination failures, contingency procedures, manual takeover scenarios), (3) System-Specific Knowledge (completion of type-training for designated UAS model covering launch/recovery, autonomy modes, C2 management, emergency procedures). Evidence shall be documented and reviewed by CAA Licensing Inspector.

title: Crew competency evidence types
text_ieee830: Competency evidence shall consist of four types: (1) Training Records (signed certificates from approved training organization, dated within 2 years), (2) Written Examination (scored assessment minimum 80% pass rate covering swarm operations, contingency procedures, system knowledge), (3) Simulator Practice (minimum 10 logged practice sessions in HITL simulator per competency domain, each with instructor evaluation), (4) Flight Experience (logbook entries documenting BVLOS or swarm flights, signed by supervising pilot if required by regulatory authority). CAA Licensing Inspector shall review all four types and issue permit (approved), conditional permit (requires remedial training), or deny (material gaps).

title: Regulatory apportionment matrix
text_ieee830: For trials involving classified MOD evidence or military applications, applicant and CAA shall jointly develop Regulatory Apportionment Matrix specifying: (1) which requirements each authority owns (CAA: airspace segregation, civilian safety, public notification; MAA: security classification, military airspace, sensitive technology export control), (2) decision dependencies (which authority approves first, approval sequence), (3) information barriers (which evidence is classified vs. shareable), (4) escalation procedures (if CAA and MAA requirements conflict, escalate to executive decision level). Matrix shall be reviewed and signed by designated CAA and MAA representatives confirming gap-free coverage (every requirement REQ001-REQ020 assigned to at least one authority).

title: Phased trial structure
text_ieee830: Trial shall be structured in three reversible phases controlled by CAA Programme Manager: Phase 1 (VLOS Baseline) — single aircraft, visual control, crew within 500m. Gate criteria: ConOps approved, SORA assessment, insurance, crew credentials. Phase 2 (BVLOS Single) — 2+ aircraft, autonomous waypoint navigation, C2 link reliance. Gate criteria: Phase 1 success (minimum 10 flights, zero critical incidents), BVLOS-specific equipment validation, C2 link 99.5% uptime demonstrated. Phase 3 (BVLOS Swarm) — full multi-aircraft coordinated operations, inter-vehicle link coordination, distributed decision-making. Gate criteria: Phase 2 success (minimum 20 flights, zero unrecovered off-nominal events), swarm decision-making validation, emergent-behavior evidence bounding undesired behaviors to acceptable probability.

title: Phase exit criteria and reversal
text_ieee830: Phase exit/reversal triggered by: (1) >= 2 critical incidents in single phase (loss of separation <100m, uncontrolled off-nominal event crew unable to recover, containment failure), (2) failure of phase exit criteria (e.g., Phase 1 requires zero incidents in 10 flights; observing 2+ incidents triggers reversal), (3) uncontrolled emergent behavior detected (swarm deadlock, formation oscillation, Byzantine cascade). Upon reversal, Programme Manager shall: suspend flight operations immediately, conduct root-cause analysis, identify contributing factors, document remediation plan (design change, retraining, equipment replacement), execute remediation, and conduct re-entry testing before attempting same phase again.

title: Landowner notification
text_ieee830: Applicant shall identify property owners within 2 km of trial area (via land registry) and deliver written notice minimum 30 days prior to trial start. Notice shall explain: trial objectives, aircraft specifications, estimated noise levels, flight duration, safety measures, emergency contact information. Notification shall be delivered via certified mail with proof-of-delivery signature required. Applicant shall document all notifications and proof-of-delivery certificates for CAA review.

title: Community consultation
text_ieee830: Applicant shall conduct community consultation through: (1) public information session (or online information portal if remote area) describing trial objectives, safety measures, flight schedule, anticipated noise and visual impact, or (2) community liaison hotline accepting questions and concerns. Consultation period shall be minimum 14 days after notice delivery. Applicant shall document all concerns received and responses provided. CAA Stakeholder Engagement Lead shall review consultation documentation to verify CAP 1616 compliance.

title: Media relations and press release
text_ieee830: Applicant shall issue press release to local media (5+ business days before trial start) describing trial objectives, aircraft, safety measures, regulatory oversight, and designated media contact for questions. Applicant shall prepare response templates for anticipated media questions (noise, safety, privacy, environmental impact). Single media contact shall be designated and trained to provide consistent messaging.

title: GDPR Privacy Notice publication
text_ieee830: Applicant shall publish GDPR Article 13 Privacy Notice describing: personal data collected (crew names, observer identities, potentially incidental recording of nearby ground personnel), processing purposes (safety investigation, regulatory oversight, technical analysis), data-retention periods (2+ years or until investigation closes), data-subject rights (access, deletion after investigation closes), CAA Data Protection Officer contact. Privacy Notice shall be posted on Workbench portal and provided to all trial participants and affected ground parties.

title: Security Classification Handling Plan (SCHP) creation
text_ieee830: When trial involves classified MOD evidence, applicant and MAA shall jointly develop Security Classification Handling Plan per CAP 722H (CAA Security Management) and JSP 440 (UK Armed Forces Security Classification Policy). SCHP shall specify: (1) classification levels per evidence element (e.g., swarm de-confliction algorithm = OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE, MOD sensor specifications = SECRET), (2) information barriers (what evidence CAA can access unclassified vs. what requires SECRET facility and clearance), (3) redaction rules (which documents shall be redacted for CAA review), (4) facility requirements (MOD secure facility location, accreditation level), (5) personnel clearances required, (6) escalation if CAA deems safety-critical evidence restricted.

title: Parallel classified/unclassified assessment
text_ieee830: CAA shall designate Classified Evidence Reviewer (possessing required SECRET or TOP SECRET clearance) to conduct parallel assessment: (1) Classified Technical Review (full review of MOD evidence in secure facility, complete assessment of swarm autonomy, sensor integration, military interdependencies), (2) Unclassified Summary (Reviewer produces unclassified summary of findings and approval rationale safe for public record, without disclosing classified technical details). Trial authorization decision shall be issued based on unclassified summary; classified assessment remains confidential to authorized personnel only.

title: Environmental noise assessment
text_ieee830: Applicant shall commission noise assessment measuring or modeling predicted noise levels (dB(A)) at sensitive receptors (residences, schools, hospitals) within 5 km. Assessment shall address: (1) daytime operations (0700-2200) — target maximum 75 dB(A) at residential receivers per Environmental Protection Act 1990, (2) night operations (2200-0700) — target maximum 65 dB(A). If predicted noise exceeds limits, applicant shall propose mitigation: flight-path adjustment, noise baffles, operational restrictions (daylight hours only, altitude ceiling to reduce ground-noise impact), or acceptance of higher noise with community notification and compensation.

title: Species disturbance assessment
text_ieee830: Applicant shall commission ecological assessment identifying sensitive species (birds, bats, other protected fauna) within 10 km using published ecology databases and expert consultation. Assessment shall identify: (1) breeding seasons and dusk/night activity periods (when disturbance risk is highest), (2) nesting/foraging habitat locations (particularly for protected or endangered species), (3) UAS noise/motion disturbance impact per wildlife sensitivity guidance. If disturbance risk is elevated (e.g., active nest site within 1 km, bat foraging habitat within 2 km during breeding season), applicant shall propose mitigation (seasonal flight restrictions, dusk/night flight ban, artificial shelter deployment) or obtain Natural England license for unavoidable disturbance.

title: Battery fire risk assessment
text_ieee830: Applicant shall assess lithium battery fire risk from UAS battery or fuel leakage on ground. Assessment shall quantify: battery cell capacity (Wh), thermal runaway risk probability, potential fire-spread radius given fuel quantity on aircraft. Applicant shall require certification that batteries meet IEC 62619 (lithium safety standard) and shall document emergency fire-suppression procedures (type of extinguisher, crew training on use). Applicant shall confirm crash-landing zones (planned emergency-landing locations) are clear of vegetation/structures where fire could spread uncontrolled.

title: Lessons-learned workshop
text_ieee830: Upon trial completion, applicant shall convene Lessons-Learned Workshop within 30 days, including CAA, applicant, crew, and independent technical reviewer. Workshop shall review: (1) all incidents/anomalies logged during trial, (2) unexpected behaviors observed (emergent swarm behaviors, crew workload surprises, equipment failures), (3) deviations from ConOps, (4) regulatory gate performance (were gates meaningful? Did they catch issues?). Workshop shall produce written lessons-learned report (minimum 10 pages) documenting findings, root causes, and recommendations for future BVLOS swarm trials.

title: Evidence preservation and archival
text_ieee830: All recorded evidence (telemetry, video, audio, autonomy logs per REQ012) shall be preserved in digital archive: (1) format standardization (CSV for telemetry, MP4 for video, WAV for audio, JSON for decision logs), (2) metadata documentation (flight date, aircraft ID, crew identifiers, trial phase), (3) integrity verification (SHA256 hash of all files, stored separately), (4) retention period: minimum 2 years or until any litigation/investigation closes, whichever is longer. Archive shall be transferred to CAA Safety Investigation department for long-term storage and future reference.

title: Technical debrief and forensic validation
text_ieee830: Independent technical reviewer (not involved in trial) shall conduct forensic analysis of recorded evidence to validate: (1) swarm behavior remained within predicted bounds per architecture specification (REQ008), (2) human control was maintained per meaningful-control criteria (REQ010), (3) no safety-critical near-miss scenarios were missed (events detected and logged that could have escalated to accidents). Reviewer shall issue forensic-analysis report documenting findings and confidence level (high/medium/low) in each validation.

title: Authorization close-out notice
text_ieee830: CAA Programme Manager shall issue formal Authorization Close-Out Notice specifying: (1) trial authorization is no longer valid and aircraft shall cease operations in trial configuration, (2) evidence has been received and preserved in CAA archive, (3) if applicable, recommendations for future BVLOS swarm trials (e.g., 'swarm de-confliction algorithm validated; acceptable for expanded swarm size in future trials', or 'emergent-behavior deadlock observed; future swarm designs must include deadlock-detection mechanism'). Close-out notice shall be signed by Programme Manager and filed in permanent trial record.

title: Case cycle time target
text_ieee830: Workbench shall enforce case-cycle-time target 60 days from submission to authorization decision. Timeline: (1) days 1-5: applicant submission and Quick Gate (FR-003); (2) days 6-15: Thorough Gate (FR-004) and case opening; (3) days 16-40: SORA, safety-case, autonomy reviews (parallel); (4) days 41-50: apportionment negotiation and panel consensus (REQ015-016); (5) days 51-60: authorization approval and public notification (REQ017). If case requires RFC (return for clarification), clock resets upon resubmission. Programme Manager shall monitor case cycle time and escalate any cases exceeding 75 days (25% buffer) to CAA management.

title: Workbench availability SLA
text_ieee830: Workbench service shall maintain 99.5% availability (uptime target) during CAA business hours (0900-1700 UK time, Monday-Friday). Availability is measured across all functional components (intake, case management, review interface, decision logging). Planned maintenance windows shall be announced 2 weeks in advance and shall not exceed 4 hours per quarter. Unplanned outages exceeding 2 hours shall be reported to CAA Director within 24 hours with root-cause analysis.

title: Evidence retrieval SLA
text_ieee830: Archive service shall support evidence retrieval with SLAs: (1) metadata query (find flight record by date/aircraft/location) returns results within 1 second, (2) evidence retrieval (download telemetry/video/logs) completes within 10 seconds for files <100 GB, (3) forensic analysis access (support post-incident investigation with full evidence access) within 2 hours of request. Compliance with SLAs shall be measured monthly and reported to CAA Director.
